title: World Cup Final 2026: 8 Things You May Have Missed On TV
summary: As the World Cup concluded with a historic first-ever halftime show performance, here are several things you may have missed while watching on TV.
body: After a day of wildfire smoke that choked the air and thunderstorms that flooded parts of New York City, Sunday, July 19th dawned bright, clear and beautiful, the perfect setting for an event on which all eyes around the globe would be centered: the 2026 World Cup Final. And with Spain and Argentina contesting the first all-Spanish-language final since the inaugural 1930 event (in which Uruguay bested Argentina), one word filled the air more than any other as fans began to mass around NYC’s Penn Station to take the trains or buses to New York, New Jersey stadium for the game: Vamos! On the way to the stadium for the biggest — and most expensive — game of this month-long World Cup, Argentinian fans were singing loudly and joyously, with a sea of Lionel Messi jerseys far out-numbering those in Spain’s traditional red, with those supporters of La Roja nearly as unanimous as their counterparts in wearing the name and number of their 19-year-old superstar Lamine Yamal, a successor to Messi at the famed club Barcelona. But after a relatively simple transit situation (a relief after repeated warnings that things could get packed and hectic) that gave way to a media credential nightmare t…

title: Gracie Abrams Announces ‘Daughter From Hell’ Bonus Tracks Available ‘For a Limited Time’
summary: She's surprising fans with two more new songs, "Out of Nowhere" and "Love Letter."
body: More new music from Gracie Abrams is arriving Sunday night (July 19). After unleashing new album Daughter From Hell on Friday, the singer-songwriter announced there are two additional tracks from the DFH sessions that fans will get to hear. Though they didn’t make the cut on the standard version of the album, “Out of Nowhere” and “Love Letter” are being made available as bonus tracks. Related Gracie Abrams’ ‘Daughter From Hell’: All 16 Tracks Ranked How Similar Was the World Cup Halftime Show to the Super Bowl? Coldplay's 'We Dance' Closes 2026 World Cup Halftime Show With The Muppets, PS22 Chorus & More The two songs will be up exclusively on iTunes Sunday night, and only “for a limited time,” Abrams shared in a social media post . Her post included her personal thoughts on the songs, as well as her gratitude for those who’ve listened to Daughter From Hell and shared positive feedback. Abrams also offered a couple selfie video clips of herself giving a very short, but playfully dramatic, preview of “Out of Nowhere” and “Love Letter.” “Hey SURPRISE,” Abrams…

title: Morgan Wallen Debuts ‘Been By Now,’ His Next Single, Live in Baltimore
summary: The country hitmaker announced the song will be released Friday (July 24).
body: Morgan Wallen revealed the title and release date of his next single on Saturday night (July 18), giving fans the first listen of “Been By Now.” Related Morgan Wallen, Garth Brooks, Taylor Swift & More Artists With Most Weeks at No. 1 on Top Country Albums Chart: Full List How Similar Was the World Cup Halftime Show to the Super Bowl? Coldplay's 'We Dance' Closes 2026 World Cup Halftime Show With The Muppets, PS22 Chorus & More Wallen played the song live for the first time at his show in Baltimore, Md., at M&T Bank Stadium and revealed it’ll officially be released on Friday (July 24), as seen in videos filmed by concertgoers. He’s currently on his Still the Problem Tour, which brings him to Ann Arbor, Mich., next weekend and then wraps in Philadelphia, Pa. Post-show, he shared a 40-second clip of the recorded version of “Been By Now” on social media . “If it was meant to be, we’d have been by now/ We wouldn’t have to keep trying to work things out,” Wallen sings on the midtempo track that seems to be about a relationship that isn’t progressing, even though “baby, we’re a …

body: All products and services featured are independently chosen by editors. However, Billboard may receive a commission on orders placed through its retail links, and the retailer may receive certain auditable data for accounting purposes. Viva España! Spain has won the 2026 FIFA World Cup , with the Spanish men’s national soccer team taking down Argentina by a score of 2-1 in Sunday’s tournament finale . Ferran Torres scored the winning goal for Spain in extra time, in a World Cup final that also saw Justin Bieber, BTS, Madonna, Shakira, Coldplay and others perform during the halftime show . Now, fans can celebrate the team’s win with official Spain World Cup winners merch online. The apparel and collectibles site Fanatics, has official Team Spain World Cup champions T-shirts, hoodies and jerseys available to buy right now. As a description teases, “The Spain National Team stands atop the soccer world after outlasting the best of the best on the sport’s biggest stage. title: Morgan Wallen, Garth Brooks, Taylor Swift & More Artists With Most Weeks at No. 1 on Top Country Albums Chart: Full List
summary: Male solo artists dominate, though three female solo artists and two groups have also achieved the feat.
body: Morgan Wallen ’s I’m the Problem leads Billboard ‘s Top Country Albums chart (dated July 25) for the 45th nonconsecutive week. This ups Wallen’s career tally of weeks at No. 1 to 231, allowing him to extend his lead as the artist with the most weeks at No. 1 in the chart’s 62-year history. Wallen’s achievement is especially impressive because he has amassed this hefty total of weeks at No. 1 in such a short amount of time. He first topped the chart on Aug. 15, 2020. Before returning to No. 1 this week, I’m the Problem spent the past 13 weeks at No. 2 behind Ella Langley ‘s Dandelion . That was the longest Wallen has been locked out of the top spot since he rose to superstar status in January 2021 with Dangerous: The Double Album . Top Country Albums originated as Hot Country Albums in the Jan. 11, 1964 issue of Billboard . Johnny Cash ’s Ring of Fire (The Best of Johnny Cash) headed the inaugural chart. The compilation was well-timed: “Ring of Fire” had headed Hot Country Songs for seven weeks the previous summer and had crossed over to reach the top 20 on the Billboard Hot 100. The chart was named Hot Country LP’s from 1968 to 1984, when vinyl …

title: Ted Lasso Sends Justin Bieber Out on the World Cup Field for ‘Everything Hallelujah’ in 2026 Halftime Show
summary: Jason Sudeikis (aka Ted Lasso) made a surprise cameo ahead of Bieber's halftime performance.
body: Justin Bieber got a pep talk from Ted Lasso (Jason Sudeikis) during the 2026 FIFA World Cup Halftime Show. In between superstar music performances at the Spain vs. Argentina match on Sunday (July 19), Lasso walked the stadium with the Apple TV+ series’ Coach Beard (Brendan Hunt) with commentary on how the entertainment was going so far — and then put Bieber on the field to serenade the world with “Everything Hallelujah.” Related Shakira, Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber & More Take Over FIFA’s First-Ever World Cup Final Halftime Show How Similar Was the World Cup Halftime Show to the Super Bowl? Coldplay's 'We Dance' Closes 2026 World Cup Halftime Show With The Muppets, PS22 Chorus & More “Coach, I gotta say, first half of halftime knocked my darn socks off,” said Lasso, as played by Sudeikis in the surprise appearance by the Ted Lasso actors at New York New Jersey Stadium (aka MetLife Stadium) in East Rutherford, N.J. “How the heck is anyone gonna follow all that?” Then the Belieber within rose: “You know what I think we need? We need some heart. You know, someone who has soul. Someone who can bring…

title: How Similar Was the World Cup Halftime Show to the Super Bowl?
summary: Did FIFA offer reheated nachos or create a fresh flavor?
body: FIFA and football; the NFL and football. The two different sports that share a name now share another thing – a star-studded, hyped-up halftime show. Halftime shows have been part of the Super Bowl since 1967, its inaugural year, but 2026 marks the first major halftime show at the World Cup, which has been going on since 1930. The change certainly has a little something to do with the fact that the final FIFA World Cup showdown takes place in the United States this year (New Jersey’s MetLife Stadium, to be exact). And Lord knows that Americans love a halftime show. Each year, the Super Bowl Halftime Show is reliably the most-watched single broadcast in the U.S., outpacing the second-most-watched broadcast by millions. So: was the FIFA World Cup Halftime Show on Sunday (July 19) basically the Super Bowl Halftime Show during a soccer game, or did it feel like a fresh, distinct event? Things certainly felt different from the start. Not to state the obvious, but the Super Bowl opens with three slow-building ballads: “The Star-Spangled Banner,” part of the Super Bowl from the start; “America the Beautiful,” sung annually since 2009 (though it was also performed in 1977, the sole year t…

title: Coldplay’s ‘We Dance’ Closes 2026 World Cup Halftime Show With The Muppets, PS22 Chorus & More
summary: Written for and debuted live at the World Cup, "We Dance" was the last song in a lively halftime show.
body: What was the last song performed during the 2026 FIFA World Cup Halftime Show? It was the live premiere of “We Dance,” an original song penned by Coldplay for the mid-game, 11-minute live music medley during the Spain vs. Argentina match that also featured performances from Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber, Shakira with Burna Boy and more. Related Shakira, Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber & More Take Over FIFA’s First-Ever World Cup Final Halftime Show BTS Light Up 2026 FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show With Dynamic ‘Dynamite’ Performance How Similar Was the World Cup Halftime Show to the Super Bowl? The World Cup field at New York New Jersey Stadium (otherwise known as MetLife Stadium) in East Rutherford, N.J., was abuzz with energy by the final number of Sunday’s (July 19) halftime show, and filled with quite a sweeping ensemble of performers. Coldplay’s Chris Martin, curator of the first-ever World Cup halftime event, led his band’s new tune “We Dance” surrounded by children — the PS22 Chorus — and with special appearances from The Muppets (who’d also popped in earlier, during a symphony performance of &#82…

title: Shakira, Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber & More Take Over FIFA’s First-Ever World Cup Final Halftime Show
summary: Burna Boy, Gustavo Dudamel, Chris Martin & the PS22 Chorus joined a tightly packed 11-minute spectacle in New Jersey.
body: The 2026 FIFA World Cup Final halftime show did exactly what FIFA hoped and exactly what soccer purists feared: It turned the most sacred match in the sport into an 11-and-a-half-minute pop fever dream. Staged Sunday (July 19) at New York New Jersey Stadium (the rebranded MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey) during the Spain-Argentina final, the first-ever halftime spectacle in the championship match moved with the logic of a playlist on shuffle and the pace of a caffeine rush, squeezing Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber, Shakira, Burna Boy, Gustavo Dudamel, a children’s chorus, the Muppets, Coldplay’s Chris Martiin, and a sky full of colorful fireworks into a blink-and-you-missed-it production that still somehow felt gigantic. If the challenge was making every second count, the show’s greatest success was understanding that subtlety had no place here. Madonna kicked things off in gloriously extra fashion, rolling in with DJ Stuart Price behind her while Brazilian legends Ronaldo and Ronaldinho appeared in a jeep with a license plate that read “Maestro,” because of course they did. From there, the show lurched — thrillingly, and at times hilariously — into Gustavo Dudam…

summary: Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber, and Shakira entertain the crowd at half-time in the World Cup final, with a special appearance from Ronaldo and Ronaldinho at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

title: BTS Light Up 2026 FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show With Dynamic ‘Dynamite’ Performance
summary: The South Korean septet also joined all the halftime performers for "We Dance," an original song written by Coldplay for the show.
body: BTS brought their explosive energy to the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show on Sunday (July 19) for a dynamic performance of their 2020 smash hit “Dynamite.” As the second halftime headliners at New York New Jersey Stadium (the rebranded MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey), BTS hit the pitch wearing coordinated red, white and black outfits. It wasn’t announced ahead of time what the South Korean septet would perform, and they went with their very first Billboard Hot 100 chart-topper “Dynamite,” which spent three weeks at No. 1 in 2020; it made sense to go with their first U.S. No. 1 as they performed on a global stage in the American stadium. title: Open letter to leaders of G7, G20, BRICS and all nations on finalizing the WHO Pandemic Agreement’s Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing annex
summary: Dear Leaders of the G7, the G20, BRICS and of all nations, We write to you together, from Geneva and from Brasília, with one shared conviction: that the world must finish what it started, and that you can help it do so. We begin not with an institution or an annex, but with a memory the whole world…

title: Safe blood supply improves as voluntary donations exceed 85%, but many people still lack access
summary: New data from the World Health Organization (WHO) show sustained progress towards safer blood supplies globally but also highlight persistent inequalities in access to safe blood and weaknesses in governance, financing and regulation of blood systems.

title: Africa CDC and WHO launch joint continental Ebola response plan
summary: The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) and the World Health Organization (WHO) today launched a joint continental preparedness and response plan on the ongoing Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus. The plan aims to raise US$ 518 million to support African countr…

title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo
summary: How Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo didn't just dominate their era. They created one.

title: Unsafe food causes 866 million illnesses and 1.5 million deaths annually, young children at highest risk
summary: Children aged less than five years face almost three times the risk of illness from unsafe food than older children and adults, according to new estimates released today by the World Health Organization (WHO).

title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
summary: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

title: WHO urges governments to protect young people from addiction to tobacco and nicotine products
summary: Worldwide, at least 40 million children aged 13–15 use tobacco products, and young people’s use of e-cigarettes and nicotine pouches continues to rise. In advance of World No Tobacco Day – 31 May – the World Health Organization (WHO) urges governments around the world to protect a new generation fr…

title: Experts convened by WHO advise on candidate treatments and vaccines for Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus
summary: In response to the current outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus occurring in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with cases also reported in Uganda, WHO convened several of its expert and advisory groups. These groups assessed potential vaccines and therapeutics for both preventio…
